LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE BOLSEGA'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED ON THE CONDITION THAT THE ADDITIONAL YEAR EMBRACED BY THE AMENDMENT WILL NOT BE A BASIS FOR DETERMINING WHETHER THE PLAINTIFF HAS EXCEEDED THE 998 OFFER. PLAINTIFFS WILL PROVIDE INFORMATION ABOUT THE WAGE LOSS CLAIM FOR THAT ADDITIONAL YEAR WITHIN TEN DAYS. THE AMENDMENT WILL NOT AFFECT THE PENDING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, MEANING THAT THE ISSUES THAT THE PARTIES HAVE ADDRESSED WILL BE DEEMED EQUALLY APPLICABLE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. RESPONSE TO DEFTS' OBJECTIONS TO ERRATA TO DECLARATION OF DARIA DIMITROFF IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S OPPOS TO DEFT'S MOTION FRO SUMMARY JDUGMENT OR, IN THE ALTENRATIVE, SUMMARY ADJDUCIATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF BOLSEGA, ADRIENNE , AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC., RENAISSANCE HOTEL OPERATING COMPANY, CHRIS STEURI AND DAVE DOLQUIST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT CONTINUED FROM JUN-06-2007 TO LAW AND MOTION AT JUN-12-2007, 1:30 PM IN DEPT. 302. MATTER ARGUED AND SUBMITTED. COURT CONTINUES THE MATTER TO JUNE 12, 2007 AT 1:30 P. M. FOR FURTHER HEARING ON THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. COUNSEL ARE TO SUBMIT ELECTRONICALLY (BY 3 P.M. ON JUNE 11, 2007) A REVISED STATEMENT OF FACTS AND BRIEF STATEMENT OF DECISION. COUNSEL ACCEPTS THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING ISSUED ON POINT FIVE OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. TENTATIVE RULING WAS: 1.) IF PLAINTIFF DID NOT APPLY FOR THE F.O.M. POSITION, HOW CAN SHE HAVE A CLAIM FOR FAILURE TO PROMOTE? (FACT 58). 2.) ASSUMING YOUNGER WORKERS RECEIVED BETTER TREATMENT, HOW DOES THAT SUPPORT THE CLAIM THAT PLAINTIFF WAS TERMINATED BECAUSE OF HER AGE AND/OR NATIONAL ORIGIN? 3.) EXPLAIN WHY DEFENDANT REDUCED PLAINTIFF'S WRITTEN WARNINGS TO VERBAL WARNINGS AFTER THE D.F.E.H. COMPLAINT. 4.) APART FROM TIMING, WHAT OTHER FACTS SUPPORT PLAINTIFF'S RETALIATION CLAIM? 5.) THERE APPEAR TO BE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO PLAINTIFF'S DUTIES AND WHETHER SHE QUALIFIES AS AN EXEMPT EMPLOYEE DESCRIBED IN PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION AT PAGES 330 - 340. 6.) AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF RECEIVED PERFORMANCE REVIEWS CONTAINING CRITICAL COMMENTS AND RECEIVED SEVERAL MEMOS DETAILING PROBLEMS WITH HER WORK. PLAINTIFF DISPUTES THE ACCURANCY OF THE REVIEWS AND MEMOS BUT NOT THE RECEIPT OF THE DOCUMENTS. ABSENT A SHOWING THAT THE REVIEWS OR MEMOS WERE MOTIVATED BY AGE OR NATIONAL ORIGIN BIAS, IS IT SUFFICIENT TO DEFEAT THE MOTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO DISPUTE THE ACCURACY OF THE CRITICISMS? JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: GENA EALES, CSR 	176
REMOVED FROM MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR JUN-25-2007 - CASE SETTLED PER PLAINTIFF. ASSIGNED TO PRETRIAL DEPT. 212 ON AUG-14-2007 AT 10:00 AM FOR DISMISSAL PER CRC 3.1385. (206)
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

Ntc Ofn Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar May-15-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE BOLSEGA'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: DARIA DIMITROFF, ESQUIRE (SIEGEL & LEWITTER, 510.452.5000) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. R. SCOTT ERLEWINE, ESQUIRE (PHILLIPS & ERLEWINE, 415.398.1000) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED ON THE CONDITION THAT THE ADDITIONAL YEAR EMBRACED BY THE AMENDMENT WILL NOT BE A BASIS FOR DETERMINING WHETHER THE PLAINTIFF HAS EXCEEDED THE 998 OFFER. PLAINTIFFS WILL PROVIDE INFORMATION ABOUT THE WAGE LOSS CLAIM FOR THAT ADDITIONAL YEAR WITHIN TEN DAYS. THE AMENDMENT WILL NOT AFFECT THE PENDING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, MEANING THAT THE ISSUES THAT THE PARTIES HAVE ADDRESSED WILL BE DEEMED EQUALLY APPLICABLE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
